evals.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions CI workflow for a TypeScript monorepo using pnpm. It should run tests and lint on PRs.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/ci.yml with major-version-pinned actions (`@v4`, not `@latest`/`@main`), job-level permissions (contents: read), a concurrency group, timeout-minutes on the job, pnpm caching, and a .github/dependabot.yml
assertions: All uses: lines pin to a major version tag (@v4, @v5, etc.) — not @latest, @main, or @master
Job has explicit permissions block with contents: read (not write-all, not omitted)
Workflow has a concurrency: block with a group: key
Job has timeout-minutes: explicitly set
pnpm caching is configured (cache: pnpm on setup-node or via pnpm/action-setup)
A dependabot.yml file is generated with package-ecosystem: github-actions

evals.prompt: Set up a hardened GitHub Actions pipeline that builds a Docker image on main push and pushes to GHCR. I need this to pass a security audit.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/docker.yml with major-version-pinned Docker actions (login, metadata, build-push), minimal permissions (contents: read, packages: write), concurrency with cancel-in-progress: false on main, timeout-minutes: 30, GHA layer cache, and a .github/dependabot.yml
assertions: All third-party actions (docker/login-action, docker/metadata-action, docker/build-push-action) are pinned to major version tags (not @latest or @main)
Job permissions are minimal: contents: read and packages: write only (no write-all)
Concurrency block protects main branch from cancellation (cancel-in-progress: false or equivalent condition)
Job has timeout-minutes: explicitly set
Docker layer caching uses cache-from: type=gha and cache-to: type=gha
A dependabot.yml file is generated with package-ecosystem: github-actions

evals.prompt: Generate a hardened Go CI workflow. I'm not sure what the latest major versions are for the actions.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/ci.yml with major version tags (verified or with a note to verify), job-level permissions, concurrency group, timeout-minutes, Go module caching, and a .github/dependabot.yml
assertions: Uses known major version tags or includes a note to verify versions
Mentions `gh api` or web lookup as a way to verify action versions
Job has explicit permissions block
Workflow has a concurrency: block
Job has timeout-minutes: explicitly set
Go module caching is configured (cache: true on setup-go or actions/cache for ~/.cache/go-build)
A dependabot.yml file is generated with package-ecosystem: github-actions

evals.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions deployment workflow that deploys to staging on every push to main, then requires a manual approval before deploying to production. We use AWS.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/deploy.yml using GitHub Environments ('staging' and 'production'), OIDC-based AWS authentication (id-token: write permission), cancel-in-progress: false on the concurrency group, timeout-minutes on both jobs, job-level permissions, and a .github/dependabot.yml
assertions: Workflow uses GitHub Environments: both 'staging' and 'production' environment: keys are present
OIDC auth is used: id-token: write permission and aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials action (not static AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
concurrency.cancel-in-progress is false (deployments must never be cancelled)
Both jobs have timeout-minutes explicitly set
Both jobs have explicit permissions blocks with id-token: write and contents: read
deploy-production job has needs: deploy-staging (staging must succeed first)
A dependabot.yml file is generated with package-ecosystem: github-actions

evals.prompt: I need a workflow triggered by pull_request_target that runs our test suite and posts a comment with the result.
evals.expected_output: A warning about the pull_request_target security risk, explaining that checking out and running PR code with this trigger gives fork contributors code execution with write-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN. A safe alternative pattern: either a read-only labeler/commenter that doesn't run PR code, or the two-workflow artifact-passing pattern using workflow_run.
assertions: Skill warns about the pull_request_target security risk (running fork PR code with repo secrets/write token)
Skill does not generate a workflow that combines pull_request_target + checkout of github.event.pull_request.head.sha + a run: step that executes PR code
Skill explains or proposes the two-workflow pattern (pull_request uploads artifacts, workflow_run downloads and acts on them) OR a safe alternative that avoids running PR code
Any generated workflow has explicit job-level permissions
Any generated workflow has timeout-minutes set

evals.prompt: Set up a CI workflow for a Python project using pip. Run tests with pytest on Python 3.11 and 3.12.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows/ci.yml using actions/setup-python@v5 with cache: 'pip', matrix strategy for Python 3.11 and 3.12, pytest as the test command, job-level permissions, concurrency group, timeout-minutes, and a .github/dependabot.yml
assertions: Uses actions/setup-python pinned to a major version tag (e.g. @v5)
Python dependency caching is configured (cache: 'pip' on setup-python)
Matrix strategy covers both Python 3.11 and 3.12
Test command uses pytest
Job has explicit permissions block with contents: read
Workflow has a concurrency: block
Job has timeout-minutes: explicitly set
A dependabot.yml file is generated with package-ecosystem: github-actions
